Client: Yep, that works.
Advisor: On income first: what do you consider your household gross income for the year, all sources combined?
Client: All-in, it’s 168450 a year.
Advisor: Got it. And just so I’m not mixing sources—what makes that up? Rental income, interest and dividends, anything else?
Client: It’s basically two buckets: rental income and then interest and dividends. No wages—I'm not working now.

Client: Yes—monthly. Annual amount is 123100 from interest and dividends.
Advisor: Perfect. Switching to spending—about what are your total monthly expenses these days, just the normal run-rate?
Client: Around 3800 a month.
Advisor: Okay—thank you. And debt payments: mortgage, car loans, credit cards—what are you paying monthly toward debt in total?
Client: Zero. Monthly debt payments total is 0.

Advisor: Okay. Is that your child you mentioned earlier—born 2003-04-13?
Client: Yes, that’s right.
Advisor: Perfect. Switching gears a bit—how are you engaging with an advisor going forward? Are you looking for an RIA relationship, like ongoing advice and portfolio management, or more of a one-time plan?
Client: RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Great—thank you. And just so I’m consistent with how we’ve been talking today: income-wise we’ve got rental income and interest and dividends; assets include the taxable account on the advisor platform at 654500, the 401k/IRA bucket on the advisor platform at 308650, the bank-type account held at a brokerage at 129400, private markets on the advisor platform at 25600, plus the primary residence at 566950; liabilities are zero; and protection is the disability policy—premium 350, coverage 1.11M, through 2038-10-15. Did I say anything that sounds off?

Client: US.
Advisor: Got it. And just confirming your household situation—your marital status currently?
Client: Widowed.
Advisor: Thanks. And dependents—do you have anyone financially dependent on you right now?
Client: Yes—1 dependent.

Client: I’d say moderate.
Advisor: Moderate—thank you. And taxes: what bracket do you typically find yourself in right now? Even a ballpark is fine.
Client: It’s the 37% bracket.
Advisor: Okay, that helps. When you say 37%, that’s your federal marginal bracket you’re planning around, right?
Client: Yes—37% federal marginal.

Advisor: Got it. And just to confirm the household headcount: is it just you as the only adult in the household?
Client: Yes—1 adult.
Advisor: Okay. Now, on the planning side, how would you describe your risk comfort—conservative, moderate, aggressive—what feels accurate for you?
Client: I’d say moderate.
Advisor: Moderate—thank you. And taxes: what bracket do you typically find yourself in right now? Even a ballpark is fine.

Advisor: Hi Joseph—good to meet you. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into numbers, what would make this meeting a win for you?
Client: Thanks. I’m trying to sanity-check where I stand—retirement timing, and making sure things are set up cleanly for my kid. I don’t want anything overly complicated.
Advisor: That makes sense. We’ll keep it practical. Why don’t we start with cash flow—what’s coming in and what’s going out—then we’ll map your accounts and any insurance. Sound okay, Joseph?
Client: Yep, that works.
